nodes.name: BHS Specifications
nodes.description: External BHS specifications provided by the airport authority or consultant. These are the foundational technical and operational requirements that define the baggage handling system scope.

nodes.name: TSA CBIS Concept of Operation & Associated Funding Documentations
nodes.description: TSA Checked Baggage Inspection System Concept of Operations and all associated funding documentation required for project authorization and compliance.

nodes.name: TSA Latest PGDS
nodes.description: TSA's latest Planning Guide and Design Standards (PGDS) document, providing regulatory and design standards that must be adhered to throughout the project.

nodes.name: Mechanical Layout
nodes.description: Externally provided mechanical conveyor layout drawings showing the physical arrangement of BHS equipment in the facility.

nodes.name: Motor Manifest
nodes.description: Complete list of all motors in the BHS system including specifications, locations, and electrical requirements provided by the mechanical contractor.

nodes.name: Airport IT Cyber Security Requirements & Specifications
nodes.description: Cyber security requirements and specifications from the airport authority's IT department governing all networked systems within the airport.

nodes.name: Airlines IT Cyber Security Requirements & Specifications
nodes.description: Cyber security requirements and specifications from the airlines' IT departments for systems that interface with airline operations.

nodes.name: MCP Location
nodes.description: Main Control Panel location information provided by the airport, defining where primary control infrastructure will be installed.

nodes.name: Airlines IT Requirements & Specifications
nodes.description: General IT requirements and specifications from the airlines for system integration, communication protocols, and operational interfaces.

nodes.name: KASA Contracting
nodes.description: KASA (or relevant authority) contracting process and requirements that govern project execution terms and conditions.

nodes.name: Brock BID Proposal & Sale Estimates
nodes.description: Brock's formal BID proposal and sales estimates developed from the BHS specifications, TSA documents, and mechanical layout. This document initiates the sales and contracting process.

nodes.name: Contracting & NTP
nodes.description: Contract execution and issuance of Notice to Proceed (NTP). This milestone authorizes the project team to begin engineering and development activities.

nodes.name: Internal Project Kick-off
nodes.description: Internal project kick-off meeting that formally initiates the project. Triggers work order creation in Brock's project management system and resource planning.

nodes.name: Brock Report Work Order
nodes.description: Formal work order created in Brock's reporting system to track the project, assign budget codes, and enable time and resource tracking across all disciplines.

nodes.name: Resource Assignment
nodes.description: Assignment of engineering, controls, IT, and other resources to the project based on project scope and schedule requirements.

nodes.name: SharePoint Setup
nodes.description: Setup of the project SharePoint site for document management, collaboration, and file sharing among all project disciplines and stakeholders.

nodes.name: Preliminary Internal Schedule
nodes.description: Initial project schedule developed internally, capturing key milestones, phase durations, and discipline dependencies across the full project lifecycle.

nodes.name: Panel Build List
nodes.description: Consolidated list of all control and power panels to be fabricated, used by the panel shop to schedule and track panel build activities.

nodes.name: 10% Design
nodes.description: 10% design deliverable from the external engineer of record or consultant. Provides early schematic-level design for internal review and planning.

nodes.name: 70% Design
nodes.description: 70% design deliverable providing near-complete engineering drawings and specifications for detailed internal engineering and procurement initiation.

nodes.name: 100% Design
nodes.description: 100% final design package from the external engineer of record. Fully approved and stamped drawings used for construction, fabrication, and regulatory submittals.

nodes.name: Internal Schedule Validation
nodes.description: Cross-discipline review and validation of the preliminary internal schedule, ensuring all teams agree on milestones, durations, and dependencies.

nodes.name: HMI Software Platform Selection
nodes.description: Selection of the HMI software platform (e.g., FactoryTalk, Ignition) based on project requirements, airport standards, and compatibility with the control architecture.

nodes.name: SmartSort Version Selection
nodes.description: Selection of the SmartSort upper-level software version based on project requirements, system architecture, and compatibility with existing airport infrastructure.

nodes.name: HMI Functional Specification Sections
nodes.description: HMI-specific sections of the project functional specification, defining screen layouts, navigation, alarms, reporting, and user interaction requirements.

nodes.name: SmartSort Functional Specification Sections
nodes.description: SmartSort upper-level software sections of the functional specification, defining sortation logic, bag tracking, carrier integration, and statistical reporting requirements.

nodes.name: IT Functional Specification Sections
nodes.description: IT-specific sections of the project functional specification covering network architecture, cybersecurity, server infrastructure, and system integration requirements.

nodes.name: Controls Functional Specification Sections
nodes.description: Controls-specific sections of the project functional specification defining PLC architecture, device-level control logic, safety systems, and inter-system communication.

nodes.name: Brock Functional Specifications for Approval
nodes.description: Consolidated Brock functional specification document compiled from all discipline sections, submitted to the airport/client for review and approval.

nodes.name: Approved Functional Specification
nodes.description: Client/airport authority approved functional specification. This approval gates the start of the formal development phase across all disciplines.

nodes.name: Upper-level Architecture Design, Review & Modification
nodes.description: Design, review, and modification of the upper-level network and system architecture including server topology, network segmentation, and system interconnections.

nodes.name: Cyber Security Specification
nodes.description: Detailed cybersecurity specification documenting network security controls, access management, firewall rules, patching policies, and compliance requirements.

nodes.name: Logical Network Design
nodes.description: Logical network design document defining VLANs, subnets, routing protocols, and traffic segmentation for the BHS control and upper-level network infrastructure.

nodes.name: Upper-level Catalog Cut
nodes.description: Catalog cut sheets for all upper-level hardware including servers, workstations, network switches, and associated equipment selected for the project.

nodes.name: Switch Typology/Connection Design
nodes.description: Detailed switch topology and connection design document specifying port assignments, VLAN configurations, uplink connections, and redundancy paths for all network switches.

nodes.name: Switch Connection Spreadsheet
nodes.description: Spreadsheet documenting all switch port connections, device assignments, VLAN memberships, and IP addresses for every switch in the BHS network.

nodes.name: BHS Upper-level Network IP Spreadsheet
nodes.description: Complete IP address allocation spreadsheet for all devices on the BHS upper-level network including servers, workstations, PLCs, cameras, and infrastructure equipment.

nodes.name: Upper-level Network Drawing for Approval
nodes.description: Formal upper-level network drawing submitted for client/airport authority approval, showing all network components, connections, and IP addressing.

nodes.name: IT BOM
nodes.description: Complete IT bill of materials listing all hardware, software licenses, and third-party components required for the upper-level BHS network and server infrastructure.

nodes.name: Upper-level Hardware & Third-party Software Licensing Procurement
nodes.description: Procurement of all approved upper-level hardware (servers, switches, workstations) and third-party software licenses required for the BHS upper-level systems.

nodes.name: Server Hardware & Switch Office Setup
nodes.description: Physical setup and rack assembly of server hardware and network switches in the office/staging environment for pre-configuration and testing prior to site delivery.

nodes.name: Server Hardware & Switch QA
nodes.description: Quality assurance inspection and testing of all server hardware and network switches to verify hardware functionality, configuration accuracy, and readiness for software installation.

nodes.name: Software Installation & Setup
nodes.description: Installation and configuration of all required software on servers and workstations including operating systems, middleware, database software, and BHS application software.

nodes.name: Workstation Configuration, Installation, Setup & Imaging
nodes.description: Configuration, OS imaging, and setup of all operator and engineering workstations including application installation, network configuration, and security hardening.

nodes.name: IT Infrastructure Setup (VLANs, VMs, and etc.)
nodes.description: Configuration of IT infrastructure including virtual machine setup, VLAN configuration, firewall rules, DNS, DHCP, Active Directory, and other network services.

nodes.name: Account & Password Creation in DPS
nodes.description: Creation of user accounts, service accounts, and passwords within the Distributed Processing System (DPS) and associated security management systems.

nodes.name: Lower-level Architecture Design, Review & Modification
nodes.description: Design, review, and modification of the lower-level PLC and field device control architecture including panel layout, device distribution, and network topology.

nodes.name: Control/Power Panel Breakout
nodes.description: Breakdown and assignment of all control zones to specific control and power panels, defining which devices are controlled by each panel throughout the BHS.

nodes.name: PLC Breakout
nodes.description: Assignment of PLC processors and I/O to control panels, defining the PLC program structure, chassis configuration, and inter-PLC communication paths.

nodes.name: Lower-level Catalog Cut
nodes.description: Catalog cut sheets for all lower-level electrical components including motor starters, VFDs, breakers, terminals, conduit, and field devices selected for the project.

nodes.name: Lower-level Hardware Estimator
nodes.description: Detailed hardware quantity and cost estimate for all lower-level controls components including PLCs, I/O cards, field devices, and panel hardware.

nodes.name: Power Calculation
nodes.description: Electrical power calculations for all BHS equipment including motor loads, panel sizing, wire gauge selection, and power distribution design.

nodes.name: Single Line Drawing For Approval
nodes.description: Single-line electrical drawing submitted for client/engineer of record approval, showing power distribution from the main distribution panel to all sub-panels and major loads.

nodes.name: Flattened 2D CAD Conveyor Layout
nodes.description: Flattened 2D CAD drawing of the conveyor layout used as the base layer for electrical device placement, E-stop layout, and field wiring design.

nodes.name: Device/E-stop Layout Drawing for Approval
nodes.description: Drawing showing the location of all field devices and E-stop push buttons throughout the BHS, submitted for client approval prior to construction.

nodes.name: Device/E-stop Layout for Construction
nodes.description: Approved and released device and E-stop layout drawing used by field installers for device mounting and wiring during BHS construction.

nodes.name: Control Station Typicals Drawing for Approval
nodes.description: Typical drawings for all control station types (local control stations, E-stop stations, junction boxes) submitted for client/engineer approval.

nodes.name: HMI Tag Convention
nodes.description: Document defining the naming convention for all HMI tags, ensuring consistent tag names across the PLC program, HMI screens, and upper-level systems.

nodes.name: HMI Color & Animation Scheme
nodes.description: Specification defining the color scheme and animation standards for all HMI objects, ensuring consistent visual feedback for operators across all HMI screens.

nodes.name: HMI Controls
nodes.description: Definition of standard HMI control objects (faceplates, navigation buttons, alarm displays) used consistently throughout all HMI screens in the project.

nodes.name: PLC IO Tag Convention
nodes.description: Document defining the naming convention for all PLC I/O tags, ensuring consistent and logical naming across all PLC programs and I/O modules in the system.

nodes.name: Lower-level IO and Peer to Peer Network Design
nodes.description: Design of the lower-level I/O network and PLC peer-to-peer communication network, defining network topology, protocols, and data exchange between PLCs.

nodes.name: Network Drawing for Approval
nodes.description: Formal network drawing combining upper and lower-level network design, submitted for client/engineer of record approval prior to fabrication.

nodes.name: Control Panel IO Sheets
nodes.description: I/O assignment sheets for each control panel listing every I/O point, its tag name, device assignment, and wire number for use in panel design and PLC programming.

nodes.name: Control & Power Panel Drawing for Approval
nodes.description: Complete control and power panel drawings submitted for client and engineer of record approval, showing panel layout, wiring diagrams, and component specifications.

nodes.name: Logical Control Panel Drawing for Approval
nodes.description: Logical control panel drawings showing PLC I/O wiring, peer-to-peer connections, and network device connections, submitted for approval.

nodes.name: Control & Power Panel Drawing for Fabrication
nodes.description: Approved and released control and power panel drawings issued to the panel shop for fabrication, including all revisions from the approval process.

nodes.name: Logical Control Panel Drawing for Fabrication
nodes.description: Approved and released logical control panel drawings issued to the panel shop for fabrication of network and peer-to-peer wiring.

nodes.name: Single Line Drawing For Construction
nodes.description: Approved single-line drawing released for construction use by field electricians, including all approval markups and final revisions.

nodes.name: Control & Power Panel Drawing for Construction
nodes.description: Approved control and power panel drawings released for construction reference, used by field team for installation verification and as-built mark-ups.

nodes.name: Logical Control Panel Drawing for Construction
nodes.description: Approved logical control panel drawings released for construction use by field installers for network and PLC wiring verification.

nodes.name: Network Drawing for Construction
nodes.description: Approved network drawing released for field construction use, guiding installation of network cabling, switch mounting, and device connections.

nodes.name: PDP Quote and Lead Time
nodes.description: Power Distribution Panel (PDP) quote and lead time obtained from suppliers to inform procurement scheduling and project timeline.

nodes.name: PDP Procurement
nodes.description: Formal procurement/purchase order for the Power Distribution Panel(s) based on approved specifications and confirmed lead times.

nodes.name: PDP Inspection
nodes.description: Factory inspection of the Power Distribution Panel at the manufacturer's facility to verify compliance with specifications before shipment.

nodes.name: PDP Site Delivery
nodes.description: Delivery of the approved Power Distribution Panel to the project site, ready for installation by the electrical contractor.

nodes.name: Control, Power Logical Panels & Field Device Material Procurement
nodes.description: Procurement of all materials required for control panels, power panels, logical panels, and field devices including all components listed on approved drawings and BOM.

nodes.name: Controls Development Task Scheduler
nodes.description: Detailed task schedule for the controls development team, breaking down PLC programming, testing, and documentation activities into assignable work packages.

nodes.name: PLC Logic Template
nodes.description: Reusable PLC logic template library providing standardized function blocks and routines for common BHS devices (conveyors, diverters, scanners) used in program development.

nodes.name: Source Generator Sheets
nodes.description: Structured spreadsheets used to automatically generate PLC source code, tag databases, and HMI configurations from device lists and I/O assignments.

nodes.name: Upper-level Tags Export
nodes.description: Export of upper-level tag database from the Controls source generator, providing tag definitions for SmartSort and HMI upper-level system configuration.

nodes.name: HMI Tag Exports
nodes.description: Export of HMI tag database from the Controls source generator, providing all tag definitions required for HMI screen development and PLC connection configuration.

nodes.name: IO Simulator
nodes.description: I/O simulation tool that allows PLC programs to be tested offline without physical hardware, simulating field device inputs and verifying output logic.

nodes.name: Device-level Test Plan Data Exports
nodes.description: Automated data exports from the source generator system providing device lists, tag assignments, and configuration data used to generate device-level test plans.

nodes.name: Office Testing Setup
nodes.description: Setup of the office testing environment including PLC hardware, network connections, simulation software, and HMI stations for pre-site testing activities.

nodes.name: PLC Program
nodes.description: Complete PLC program developed from the logic templates and source generator sheets, implementing all conveyor control, safety, and communication logic for the BHS.

nodes.name: Control Development Plan
nodes.description: Comprehensive controls development plan documenting the approach, timeline, and milestones for PLC programming, testing, and commissioning activities.

nodes.name: SmartSuite Software Auto-deployment
nodes.description: Automated deployment of SmartSuite software to all designated servers and workstations using the configured IT infrastructure and deployment tools.

nodes.name: Statistic Configuration
nodes.description: Configuration of the SmartSort statistical reporting module, defining KPIs, data collection points, report templates, and dashboard elements for BHS performance monitoring.

nodes.name: HMI Alarm Mapping
nodes.description: Mapping of PLC and device alarms to the SmartSort HMI alarm management system, defining alarm priorities, display text, and notification routing.

nodes.name: Device ID Configuration
nodes.description: Configuration of unique device identifiers within SmartSort for all BHS components, enabling bag tracking, device status monitoring, and event correlation.

nodes.name: Upper-level Services
nodes.description: Configuration and startup of all SmartSort upper-level Windows services required for system operation including bag tracking, statistics, alarming, and communication services.

nodes.name: Web Client Configurations
nodes.description: Configuration of SmartSort web client interfaces providing browser-based monitoring and control access for authorized airport operators and maintenance personnel.

nodes.name: PRTG Configurations
nodes.description: Configuration of PRTG network monitoring software to monitor all BHS servers, workstations, network switches, and services, with alerting for outages and performance issues.

nodes.name: Upper-level Tag Exports
nodes.description: Export of all upper-level tags from SmartSort configuration for use in integration testing, HMI configuration verification, and system documentation.

nodes.name: External Feed Connection Configuration
nodes.description: Configuration of SmartSort connections to external airline and airport systems including DCS feeds, check-in system interfaces, and FIDS integrations.

nodes.name: PLC Connection Configuration & Validation
nodes.description: Configuration and validation of SmartSort communication connections to all PLCs in the BHS, verifying data exchange, tag mapping, and status monitoring.

nodes.name: HMI Servers Software Installation & Setup
nodes.description: Installation and initial configuration of HMI server software on designated servers, including license activation, server redundancy setup, and initial system configuration.

nodes.name: HMI Conveyor Layout
nodes.description: Creation of the conveyor layout background graphic in the HMI system, providing the base map for device placement and operational overview screens.

nodes.name: Device Animation
nodes.description: Development of animated device objects in the HMI that visually indicate device states (running, stopped, faulted, in auto/manual) based on PLC tag values.

nodes.name: Device Placement
nodes.description: Placement of animated device objects onto the HMI conveyor layout graphic at locations matching the physical BHS installation.

nodes.name: PLC Tag Import
nodes.description: Import of PLC tag database into the HMI server, establishing the tag references used by all HMI screens, faceplates, and data logging functions.

nodes.name: PLC Connection Configuration
nodes.description: Configuration of HMI server connections to all PLCs in the BHS, establishing communications paths and verifying successful data exchange.

nodes.name: HMI QA
nodes.description: Quality assurance review of all HMI screens, device animations, tag bindings, and navigation to verify correctness and compliance with the HMI functional specification.

nodes.name: UL Tag Import
nodes.description: Import of upper-level (SmartSort) tags into the HMI server, enabling display of sortation data, bag counts, and upper-level system status on HMI screens.

nodes.name: Finishing up HMI Connection Screen
nodes.description: Final development and completion of HMI connection monitoring screens showing PLC and upper-level system connection status, enabling rapid fault diagnosis.

nodes.name: Connection Screen QA
nodes.description: Quality assurance review of HMI connection screens, verifying all PLC and upper-level connections are correctly displayed and status indicators function properly.

nodes.name: Device-level Test Plan Template
nodes.description: Standardized template for device-level test plans defining the format, required test steps, pass/fail criteria, and sign-off requirements for all device types in the BHS.

nodes.name: Sample Device-level Test Plan
nodes.description: Completed sample device-level test plan created from the template using actual project device data, used for client review and approval of the testing approach.

nodes.name: Sample Device-level Test Plan Review
nodes.description: Controls team review of the sample device-level test plan to verify completeness, accuracy, and alignment with PLC programming and device configuration.

nodes.name: Device-level Test Plan Generation
nodes.description: Automated generation of device-level test plans for all BHS devices using the approved template and data exports from the controls source generator system.

nodes.name: Panel & Field Device Fabrication
nodes.description: Fabrication of all control panels, power panels, and field device assemblies in the panel shop according to approved drawings and procurement materials.

nodes.name: Panel Shop QA
nodes.description: Quality assurance inspection of fabricated panels and field device assemblies, verifying compliance with drawings, correct component installation, and wiring accuracy.

nodes.name: Engineering Testing
nodes.description: Engineering-level testing of fabricated panels in the panel shop, including PLC I/O verification, communication testing, and functional logic checks prior to shipping.

nodes.name: Label Engraving
nodes.description: Engraving of identification labels for all panel components, devices, terminals, and field equipment according to the tag naming conventions and drawing requirements.

nodes.name: Shipping
nodes.description: Shipping of completed and tested panels and field device assemblies from the panel shop to the project site.

nodes.name: Panel & Field Device Site Delivery
nodes.description: Receipt of panels and field devices at the project site, including unloading, staging, and coordination with site general contractor for installation.

nodes.name: Emulation Layout
nodes.description: Creation of the BHS conveyor layout within the emulation software, providing the physical model basis for virtual commissioning and testing activities.

nodes.name: PLC Tag Configurations
nodes.description: Configuration of PLC tag connections within the emulation software, linking the virtual BHS model to the PLC program for real-time simulation testing.

nodes.name: Field Device Creation and Placement
nodes.description: Creation and placement of virtual field devices (sensors, actuators, E-stops) within the emulation model at locations matching the physical BHS layout.

nodes.name: PLC Emulation Logic
nodes.description: Development of emulation logic within the simulation software that accurately models conveyor physics, bag movement, scanner reads, and device behaviors for PLC testing.

nodes.name: Emulation Model Integration
nodes.description: Integration of the emulation model with the PLC program, enabling virtual commissioning where the PLC controls the simulated BHS and results are verified.

nodes.name: Tracking Stats and Window Sheets
nodes.description: Development of tracking statistics and time window configuration sheets defining baggage flow parameters, timing windows, and statistical targets for system optimization.

nodes.name: Tracking Setup
nodes.description: Configuration of the bag tracking system including tracking zones, handoff points, read point assignments, and tracking algorithm parameters within the BHS.

nodes.name: Device Setup
nodes.description: Individual device setup and parameter configuration for all BHS devices including speed settings, timer values, tracking parameters, and operational mode configurations.

nodes.name: System Testing
nodes.description: Comprehensive system-level testing using the integrated PLC program, HMI, emulation model, and SmartSort upper-level software to verify end-to-end BHS functionality.

nodes.name: ATR & BMA Integration Specifications
nodes.description: Specifications for integration of Automatic Tag Reader (ATR) and Baggage Management Application (BMA) systems, defining data exchange formats, timing, and protocols.

nodes.name: SMH & SAM Connection
nodes.description: Configuration and testing of connections between the BHS controls and the Sort Management Host (SMH) and Sort Assignment Module (SAM) for real-time sortation decisions.

nodes.name: HMI Issue List
nodes.description: Compiled list of HMI issues identified during system testing, providing a tracked action item list for HMI development team resolution prior to FAT.

nodes.name: Device Level Testing Issue List Resolution
nodes.description: Resolution by the HMI team of all device-level testing issues identified in the Controls HMI issue list, with verification sign-off for each corrected item.

nodes.name: HMI Server Licensing & Redundancy Setup
nodes.description: Finalization of HMI server licensing activation and configuration of server redundancy/failover mechanisms to ensure high availability of the HMI system.

nodes.name: Functional Office Testing
nodes.description: Full functional office testing of the integrated BHS control system including PLC program, HMI, SmartSort upper-level, and emulation in the office environment.

nodes.name: Lower-level Factory Acceptance Test Plan
nodes.description: Formal factory acceptance test plan for the lower-level PLC and controls system, defining test procedures, acceptance criteria, and sign-off requirements.

nodes.name: Upper-level Factory Acceptance Test Plan
nodes.description: Formal factory acceptance test plan for the SmartSort upper-level system, defining test scenarios, data validation procedures, and acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: IT Factory Acceptance Test Plan
nodes.description: Formal factory acceptance test plan for the IT infrastructure, covering network connectivity, server performance, VLAN segmentation, redundancy failover, and security controls.

nodes.name: Cyber FAT Requirement Decision
nodes.description: Decision point determining whether a formal Cyber Security Factory Acceptance Test is required based on contract requirements, airport/airline cyber security specifications.

nodes.name: Cyber FAT Test Plan
nodes.description: Formal cybersecurity factory acceptance test plan defining penetration testing scope, vulnerability scanning procedures, and security control verification requirements.

nodes.name: Cyber FAT
nodes.description: Execution of the cybersecurity factory acceptance test including network vulnerability scanning, penetration testing, security configuration review, and results documentation.

nodes.name: Factory Acceptance Test Prep
nodes.description: Preparation activities for the Factory Acceptance Test including client notification, environment setup, test equipment calibration, and stakeholder coordination.

nodes.name: Factory Acceptance Test
nodes.description: Formal Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) conducted with the client witnessing, demonstrating full BHS functionality including controls, HMI, upper-level software, and IT infrastructure.

nodes.name: Post Factory Acceptance Test Correction
nodes.description: Resolution of all punch list items and deficiencies identified during the Factory Acceptance Test, with client re-verification of corrected items.

nodes.name: System Level Test HMI Issue List
nodes.description: Compiled list of outstanding HMI issues identified during system-level testing phase, carried forward for resolution before integrated testing.

nodes.name: System Level Test HMI Issue List Resolution
nodes.description: HMI team resolution of all system-level test HMI issues with verification and sign-off, ensuring HMI quality before integrated testing with all systems.

nodes.name: Upper Level Issue List
nodes.description: Compiled list of upper-level system issues identified during FAT and post-FAT testing, covering SmartSort, HMI, and IT infrastructure items requiring resolution.

nodes.name: Integrated Testing
nodes.description: Fully integrated testing of all BHS systems together including controls, HMI, SmartSort upper-level, ATR/BMA interfaces, and external airline/airport system connections.

nodes.name: Issue List Resolution
nodes.description: Resolution of all issues identified during integrated testing across all disciplines, with coordinated retesting to verify fixes before site activities begin.

nodes.name: Rabit7 Scan
nodes.description: Execution of Rabit7 (or equivalent) cybersecurity vulnerability scan against all BHS networked systems to identify security vulnerabilities before site deployment.

nodes.name: Rabit7 Scan Result Remediation
nodes.description: Remediation of all vulnerabilities identified in the Rabit7 cybersecurity scan, including patching, configuration changes, and re-scanning to verify remediation effectiveness.

nodes.name: Field Mark-ups & Red Lines
nodes.description: Field mark-ups and red-line drawings created by installers during site construction, capturing all deviations from issued-for-construction drawings for as-built documentation.

nodes.name: As-built Drawings
nodes.description: Final as-built drawings updated from field red-lines and mark-ups, accurately reflecting the installed BHS system configuration for client handover and future maintenance reference.
